whats_new__cod_handling_logics_for_rider.title: What’s New_ COD Handling Logics for Riders
whats_new__cod_handling_logics_for_rider.purpose: Introduce a comprehensive COD management system for controlling rider eligibility, cash limits, and enforcement actions.
whats_new__cod_handling_logics_for_rider.summary: New COD management features include eligibility-based order allocation, configurable cash limits with enforcement options, real-time alerts, and automated actions to enhance cash control and reduce operational risks.
benefits: Assign COD orders only to qualified riders
Control cash in the field with configurable limits
Choose strict blocking or partial restriction for enforcement
Real-time alerts for cash breaches
Automate enforcement to reduce manual monitoring
Improve cash reconciliation and reduce leakage risk
how_to_use: Define rider eligibility criteria (lifetime orders, recent performance)
Set cash limits (max outstanding, excess over earnings)
Configure enforcement actions (block orders, monitor only)
Enable automated alerts for threshold breaches
Assign admin tasks for cash deposit when limits are exceeded
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ COD Handling Logics for Riders.eml

whats_new__cleaner_fulfillment_mis_repor.title: What’s New_ Cleaner Fulfillment MIS Report
whats_new__cleaner_fulfillment_mis_repor.purpose: Provide precise, auditable data for billing and reconciliation when multiple fulfilment partners are involved
whats_new__cleaner_fulfillment_mis_repor.summary: Introduces a new Fulfillment MIS report that offers clean, order-level visibility into billable fulfilment transactions executed by partners, improving accuracy and reliability for finance and operations teams
benefits: Cleaner billing data with no zero-value clutter
Accurate visibility into partner fulfilment charges
Easier reconciliation for cancellations/re-bookings
Report structure aligned with finance workflows
how_to_use: Select 'Partner Fulfilment' option when downloading transactions from the Billing page
Report includes only non-zero entries for orders fulfilled by partners (3PL/PPN)
Entries reflect each fulfilment request, including re-bookings and cancellations
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Cleaner Fulfillment MIS Report.eml

whats_new__clearer_partial_delivery_flow.title: What’s New_ Clearer Partial Delivery Flows
whats_new__clearer_partial_delivery_flow.purpose: Improving clarity and data consistency in partial deliveries
whats_new__clearer_partial_delivery_flow.summary: Partial deliveries now provide complete data tracking with mandatory reasoning and custom reason mapping features
benefits: Isolate root causes of incomplete deliveries
Streamline returns with pre-defined choices
Provide clearer insights into return trends
how_to_use: Provide specific reason codes for partial deliveries
Upload custom business terminology for rider visibility
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Clearer Partial Delivery Flows.eml

whats_new__collect_shipping_charges_easi.title: What’s New_ Collect Shipping Charges easily
whats_new__collect_shipping_charges_easi.summary: Define mandatory charges or returnable crates in every order for complete control over deliveries and returns. Automatically group items, create return orders, and track tasks for riders.
benefits: Complete control over delivered items and returnable materials
Automatic grouping and tracking of orders
Structured rider task view with no modifications allowed
COD amount auto-adjustment
how_to_use: Define mandatory charges and returnable crates in order settings
Use the force_action API parameter for programmatic control
Monitor dashboard for automatic order grouping and return order creation
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Collect Shipping Charges easily.eml

whats_new__configure_surges_for_real_wor.title: What’s New_ Configure Surges for real world conditions
whats_new__configure_surges_for_real_wor.purpose: Configure surges based on real-world weather conditions to balance rider compensation and operational costs.
whats_new__configure_surges_for_real_wor.summary: Introducing Rain Surge to manage surges based on real-time weather conditions, allowing percentage and flat rate adjustments with automatic charge application.
benefits: Better rider satisfaction during tough delivery conditions
More accurate cost allocation
Reduced need for reactive adjustments
whats_new__configure_surges_for_real_wor.how_to_use: Set up Rain Surge within the Surge Pricing section by defining percentage and flat rate fields. The system will automatically apply the maximum applicable charge where overlaps occur.
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Configure Surges for real world conditions.eml

whats_new__custom_pricing_for_network_pa.title: What’s New_ Custom Pricing for Network Participants
whats_new__custom_pricing_for_network_pa.purpose: Enable network owners to set participant-specific pricing rules while maintaining network-level pricing defaults
whats_new__custom_pricing_for_network_pa.summary: Introduces Custom Rule toggle for participant-level pricing overrides with type-aware pricing views for supply/demand participants
benefits: Flexible pricing exceptions without network-wide changes
Isolated participant economics
Consistent network pricing defaults
Role-specific pricing visibility
how_to_use: Enable Custom Rule toggle for participants
Define participant-specific External/Internal prices
Configure pricing logic for service rates/surges
Ensure pricing view adapts to participant type (supply/demand)
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Custom Pricing for Network Participants.eml

whats_new__define_granular_time_based_su.title: What’s New_ Define Granular time based surges
whats_new__define_granular_time_based_su.purpose: Define granular time-based surges for precise pricing control
whats_new__define_granular_time_based_su.summary: Introduce time slot-based surges to align pricing with actual demand patterns, allowing specific start/end times and layered pricing strategies for days and dates.
benefits: Align pricing with real demand patterns
Reduce manual workarounds for short-term spikes
Cleaner and more predictable surge logic
Increased profitability through precise pricing
how_to_use: Set precise start and end times for surges
Apply flat rate or percentage pricing for time slots
Combine base surges with additional time-specific surges
Configure layered pricing for days/dates and time slots
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Define Granular time based surges.eml

whats_new__define_surges_for_waiting.title: What’s New_ Define Surges for waiting
whats_new__define_surges_for_waiting.purpose: Define charges for rider waiting time during pickup and delivery stages to improve operational transparency and pricing fairness.
whats_new__define_surges_for_waiting.summary: Introduces Waiting Charge Types for pickup and delivery, allowing networks to set time-based or status-based charges during waiting periods, aligning pricing with actual operational delays.
navigation: Flexible Charging - Time or Status Based
related: Flexible Charging - Time or Status Based
benefits: Fairness in compensating rider effort
Clarity in managing operational delays
Aligned pricing with actual operations
Improved operational efficiency
how_to_use: Define pickup waiting time rules after rider reaches pickup location until Pickup Completed
Set delivery waiting time rules after rider reaches delivery location until Delivered
Configure charges via timeframes (e.g., first 5 minutes free) or status windows (based on operational milestones)
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Define Surges for waiting.eml

whats_new__dispose_as_a_outcome_for_fail.title: What’s New_ Dispose as a Outcome for Failed Deliveries
whats_new__dispose_as_a_outcome_for_fail.module: Dispose as a Outcome for Failed Deliveries
whats_new__dispose_as_a_outcome_for_fail.purpose: Introducing 'Dispose' as a configurable outcome for failed deliveries to provide businesses with a clear, controlled option instead of forced RTOs or manual interventions.
whats_new__dispose_as_a_outcome_for_fail.summary: Dispose is a new terminal status for undelivered orders, allowing businesses to explicitly decide disposal as the default action. It eliminates ambiguity, reduces manual work, and ensures rider clarity.
benefits: Businesses can explicitly decide when to dispose orders
Networks can declare disposal support without owning the decision
Orders carry disposal intent via APIs
Riders see clear actions in the app
Reduces manual interventions and inconsistencies
how_to_use: Configure 'Dispose' as the default action for undelivered orders in business settings
Use the 'force_dispose' API parameter to enforce disposal
Set network-level configurations to support disposal
Ensure riders use the Pidge application for disposal actions
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Dispose as a Outcome for Failed Deliveries.eml

whats_new__dynamic_pickup_and_delivery_w.title: What’s New_ Dynamic Pickup and Delivery Waiting Time
whats_new__dynamic_pickup_and_delivery_w.module: Dynamic Pickup and Delivery Waiting Time
whats_new__dynamic_pickup_and_delivery_w.purpose: Allow clients to define custom waiting times for pickups and deliveries
whats_new__dynamic_pickup_and_delivery_w.summary: Enhanced routing engine with dynamic, client-defined waiting times for improved operational accuracy and real-world route duration calculations.
benefits: No More Underestimating: Riders get realistic schedules
Zero Guesswork: Embrace operational variability
Total Operational Control: Adapt to business logic
whats_new__dynamic_pickup_and_delivery_w.how_to_use: Pass specific pickup_waiting_time and delivery_waiting_time values in API requests when creating orders.
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Dynamic Pickup and Delivery Waiting Time.eml

whats_new__finance_dashboard_for_cash_ma.title: What’s New_ Finance Dashboard for Cash Management
whats_new__finance_dashboard_for_cash_ma.purpose: Holistic COD management dashboard for tracking cash flows and settlements
whats_new__finance_dashboard_for_cash_ma.summary: New dashboard for managing cash (COD) amounts with global totals, account-level tracking, settlement adjustments, and automated reporting. Access via SSO with Pidge email ID.
benefits: Real-time cash flow tracking
Automated daily reports
Centralized settlement management
Transparent transaction visibility for clients
how_to_use: Login via SSO using Pidge email ID
Access dashboard as landing page
Settle 'Due to Pidge' with received amounts and UTRs
Adjust 'Due from Pidge' with transfer details and remarks
Review automated daily reports for transaction movements
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Finance Dashboard for Cash Management.eml

whats_new__get_notified_for_order_cancel.title: What’s New_ Get Notified for Order Cancellation
whats_new__get_notified_for_order_cancel.purpose: Notify riders of order cancellations with a dedicated alert sound
whats_new__get_notified_for_order_cancel.summary: A new notification sound configuration for order cancellation events in the Rider App, allowing businesses to set a dedicated alert tone and differentiate cancellation alerts from other notifications.
navigation: Delivery Executive Settings → App Configurations → Notifications → Order Cancellations
benefits: Improve rider awareness during live orders
Reduce missed cancellation notifications
Differentiate cancellations from general alerts
Enhance operational responsiveness
Improve app usability during high-order activity
how_to_use: Navigate to Delivery Executive Settings → App Configurations → Notifications → Order Cancellations
Select a dedicated notification tone for cancellation alerts
Use sound preview/play functionality to test the alert
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Get Notified for Order Cancellation.eml

whats_new__introducing_reverse_pickups.title: What’s New_ Introducing Reverse Pickups
whats_new__introducing_reverse_pickups.purpose: Introducing Reverse Pickup Capability with structured handling for Failed Pickups and Partial Pickups
whats_new__introducing_reverse_pickups.summary: Reverse pickups are now a first-class capability, supporting creation of reverse orders, handling real-world pickup outcomes, capturing structured exception data, enforcing validation, and maintaining operational visibility. Failed and Partial Pickup scenarios are natively supported with configurable validation and clear recording of outcomes.
navigation: Reverse Pickup Handling in the Rider App
benefits: Scalable reverse logistics workflows
Structured exception data capture
Validation enforcement for accountability
Clear recording and visibility of reverse outcomes
Native support for Failed/Partial Pickup scenarios
how_to_use: Mark pickup as failed and select defined reason with OTP/Call verification
Adjust item quantities for partial pickup and select mandatory reason
Review picked vs. not-picked items
Configure custom failed pickup reasons and proof types
Define validation rules for exception records
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Introducing Reverse Pickups.eml

whats_new__map_based_routing.title: What’s New_ Map Based Routing
whats_new__map_based_routing.purpose: Introduce Map-Based Routing for visual order management and dispatching
whats_new__map_based_routing.summary: Map-Based Routing provides a visual upgrade for managing bulk orders through an interactive map interface, enabling efficient grouping, allocation, and ad-hoc dispatching of deliveries.
navigation: → Pidge Powered → Map View (top right corner)
benefits: Zero Visibility Gaps
Ad-Hoc Efficiency
whats_new__map_based_routing.how_to_use: Switch to Map View from Orders → Pidge Powered, use pin interactions for order details, construct routes dynamically with real-time visual feedback, and allocate orders to Captive Riders.
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Map Based Routing.eml

whats_new__new_order_token_controls.title: What’s New_ New Order Token Controls
whats_new__new_order_token_controls.purpose: Handing controls to manage order tokens with dynamic movement controls
whats_new__new_order_token_controls.summary: Introducing token-level management for movement and flow types to create a smarter framework for logistics operations
benefits: Total Versatility
Instant Execution
how_to_use: Select movement type via single-select dropdown (Forward/Reverse)
Configure flow type via multi-select menu for operational complexity
Apply one-click logic layers based on movement type
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ New Order Token Controls.eml

whats_new__new_reports_and_enhancements_.title: What’s New_ New Reports and Enhancements (1)
whats_new__new_reports_and_enhancements_.summary: New reporting improvements provide better delivery performance analysis, route-level visibility, and delivery attempt history tracking. Adds Route ID/Name columns to Completed tab and introduces Order Re-attempt Details report with attempt history, failure reasons, rider info, and business-specific fields for parent accounts.
benefits: Improved operational analysis
Faster identification of delivery challenges
Complete visibility into delivery journeys
Simplified reporting workflows
Enhanced fulfillment performance monitoring
how_to_use: Access Route ID/Name columns in Pidge Powered Completed Tab
Download Order Re-attempt Details report from All Orders/Pidge Powered tabs
Utilize Brand Name/Business ID fields for parent account analysis
Export complete attempt records for order delivery journey tracking
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ New Reports and Enhancements (1).eml

whats_new__new_reports_and_enhancements_.title: What’s New_ New Reports and Enhancements (2)
whats_new__new_reports_and_enhancements_.purpose: Help operations teams analyze delivery performance, gain route-level visibility, and track delivery attempt history with greater accuracy
whats_new__new_reports_and_enhancements_.summary: New reports and enhancements include route information in Pidge Powered → Completed Tab with Route ID/Name columns, and an Order Re-attempt Details report with attempt history, failure reasons, rider info, and brand/business identifiers for parent accounts
benefits: Effective operational analysis
Faster identification of delivery challenges
Improved fulfillment performance monitoring
Complete visibility into delivery journeys
whats_new__new_reports_and_enhancements_.how_to_use: Access reports in Pidge Powered → Completed Tab and All Orders/Pidge Powered tabs; utilize new Route ID/Name columns for route-based analysis; export Order Re-attempt Details report for comprehensive attempt history tracking
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ New Reports and Enhancements (2).eml

whats_new__new_reports_and_enhancements.title: What’s New_ New Reports and Enhancements
whats_new__new_reports_and_enhancements.purpose: Enhanced reporting features for operations teams to analyze delivery performance and gain route-level visibility
whats_new__new_reports_and_enhancements.summary: New reports and enhancements provide route information tracking, order re-attempt details, and comprehensive delivery attempt history analysis
benefits: Improved operational analysis
Faster identification of delivery challenges
Enhanced fulfillment performance monitoring
how_to_use: Access Route Information report in Pidge Powered → Completed Tab
Download Order Re-attempt Details report from All Orders and Pidge Powered tabs
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ New Reports and Enhancements.eml

whats_new__now_define_rto_pricing_struct.title: What’s New_ Now define RTO Pricing Structure
whats_new__now_define_rto_pricing_struct.summary: Network owners can now configure RTO charges explicitly using the same flexible pricing framework as delivery pricing, enabling structured and auditable return economics.
benefits: Predictable and consistent RTO costs
Align return economics with commercial models
Eliminate manual calculations and post-facto adjustments
how_to_use: Configure RTO charges using Distance, Pincode, Unpaid Trips, and Pricing Percentage
Apply pricing rules with the same structure as standard pricing module
Use Pricing Percentage model to calculate charges based on original delivery price
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Now define RTO Pricing Structure.eml

whats_new__one_id_to_track_everything.title: What’s New_ One ID to track Everything
whats_new__one_id_to_track_everything.purpose: Improve tracking consistency across the platform by standardizing primary identifiers
whats_new__one_id_to_track_everything.summary: Reference ID is now the universal primary identifier across all Pidge Powered pages, replacing inconsistent IDs like Order ID and Ref ID. This simplifies order tracking and reduces cognitive load for Admins.
benefits: Zero Confusion, Faster Tracking
Tailored Workspace Control
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ One ID to track Everything.eml

whats_new__order_categories.title: What’s New_ Order Categories
whats_new__order_categories.purpose: Enabling smarter routing, better operational alignment, and improved delivery outcomes
whats_new__order_categories.summary: Introducing Order Categories to define order types at business and network levels, enabling smart network mapping, API support for order creation, and serviceability control based on category.
benefits: Better Operational Alignment: Food deliveries routed to speed/perishability optimized networks; Parcel to cost/batching optimized networks
Improved Delivery Accuracy: Eliminates incorrect network selection, reduces manual intervention and routing errors
Standardized Workflow via API: External systems control categorization, ensuring seamless integration with SOPs and 3PL requirements
how_to_use: Pass order_category key with values: parcel, food during order creation API
System defaults to Parcel if category not provided
Network visibility in serviceability modal governed by Order Category
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Order Categories.eml

whats_new__pidge_mobile_dashboard.title: What’s New_ Pidge Mobile Dashboard
whats_new__pidge_mobile_dashboard.purpose: Rebranding the Pidge app as a companion mobile version of the desktop operations console
whats_new__pidge_mobile_dashboard.summary: Formally positioning the app as a companion to major operational aspects of the dashboard for seamless continuity between desktop and mobile workflows
benefits: Clear understanding of app's role as companion to operational aspects
Seamless continuity between desktop and mobile workflows
Greater confidence in managing operations from mobile
how_to_use: Download from Google Play Store link provided
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Pidge Mobile Dashboard.eml

whats_new__realtime_rider_earnings_and_i.title: What’s New_ Realtime Rider Earnings and Incentives
whats_new__realtime_rider_earnings_and_i.purpose: Introducing a unified earnings experience for riders to track completed orders, surges, and incentives in real-time.
whats_new__realtime_rider_earnings_and_i.summary: Transforming rider earnings from backend calculations into a visible, measurable experience with order-by-order breakdowns and incentive progress tracking.
related: M3M Urbana Business Park, Sector 67, Gurugram , Haryana (122101)
benefits: Transparency in payout structures
Clear visibility into per-order earnings
Real-time tracking of incentive progress
Understanding of eligibility and milestone achievements
Control over earnings visibility and motivation
how_to_use: View order details including final earning amount, reference ID, distance, and pricing indications
Track incentive progress with milestone states (Not Started/In Progress/Achieved/Missed)
Access incentive rules, eligibility, and historical data
Switch between daily and weekly earnings views
Monitor expired incentives from the last 24 hours
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Realtime Rider Earnings and Incentives.eml

whats_new__rider_active_task_webhook.title: What’s New_ Rider Active Task Webhook
whats_new__rider_active_task_webhook.purpose: Bridge the gap between dispatch and execution with real-time, event-driven visibility into your riders’ active tasks
whats_new__rider_active_task_webhook.summary: Subscribe to webhooks for structured updates on rider tasks, including real-time visibility, task sequence clarity, and SLA risk monitoring
benefits: Easily integrate rider task data into internal systems
Receive only active/ongoing tasks in webhook payloads
how_to_use: Select Rider Active Task trigger
Enable webhook configuration
Add Webhook URL and Auth Token
Test the webhook
Save configuration after successful test
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Rider Active Task Webhook.eml

whats_new__rider_breaks.title: What’s New_ Rider Breaks
whats_new__rider_breaks.purpose: Allow riders to notify businesses when they need breaks, pausing order assignments temporarily and resuming automatically to avoid delivery delays and ensure smoother operations.
whats_new__rider_breaks.summary: Rider Breaks enable riders to request short breaks (e.g., meals, rest) during shifts, with orders pausing and resuming automatically. Breaks are managed via the Executive App, with policies customizable per business and shift.
benefits: No order delays or reassignments due to rider unavailability
Reduced manual admin intervention during rider breaks
Improved rider performance metrics (e.g., acceptance rate)
Accurate rider availability tracking in allocation systems
how_to_use: Riders request breaks via Executive App: Shift Management > Request Break
Breaks are set in 5-minute increments based on shift timing
Breaks are disabled once all allocated breaks are used
Peak hours restrict break requests during high-order-volume periods
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Rider Breaks.eml
available_for: Businesses with customizable shift-based break policies

whats_new__rider_flexi_shifts_1.title: What’s New_ Rider Flexi Shifts (1)
whats_new__rider_flexi_shifts_1.purpose: Introduce a brand-new shift management model for on-demand flexibility in workforce scheduling.
whats_new__rider_flexi_shifts_1.summary: Flexi Shifts allows admins to broadcast open shift slots, enabling eligible riders to opt-in based on real-time availability, reducing manual effort and improving supply activation during demand spikes.
benefits: Reduces operational bottlenecks for admins
Empowers riders with autonomy over earning opportunities
Enables real-time supply activation during demand spikes
Minimizes manual effort in shift assignment
how_to_use: Riders can book open slots up to 4 days in advance via the Rider App
View real-time urgency tags like 'Filling Fast' or 'Almost Full'
Review shift details including order types, payment limitations, and hub distances before booking
Cancel accepted shifts instantly via the app drawer before logging in
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Rider Flexi Shifts (1).eml

whats_new__rider_flexi_shifts.title: What’s New_ Rider Flexi Shifts
whats_new__rider_flexi_shifts.purpose: Introduce a new shift management model for on-demand flexibility in workforce scheduling
whats_new__rider_flexi_shifts.summary: Flexi Shifts allows admins to broadcast open shift slots for eligible riders to opt-in based on real-time availability, addressing operational bottlenecks and enabling dynamic scheduling.
benefits: Reduces manual effort for fleet admins
Provides riders with autonomy over earning opportunities
Enables rapid supply activation during demand spikes
Enhances operational agility for unpredictable demand patterns
how_to_use: Riders can book open slots via the Rider App's Roster & Shifts tab up to 4 days in advance
Use real-time urgency tags like 'Filling Fast' to prioritize shifts
Review shift details including order types, payment terms, and hub distances before booking
Cancel shifts via the app drawer until login to return slots to the active pool
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Rider Flexi Shifts.eml

whats_new__rider_payments_receipts.title: What’s New_ Rider Payments Receipts
whats_new__rider_payments_receipts.purpose: Bring transparency and precision to every transaction done by rider
whats_new__rider_payments_receipts.summary: Introducing digital receipts for rider payments, allowing verification of transfers in the Rider App and instant access to transaction receipts for managers via web dashboard. Every payment now includes verifiable digital footprints with timestamps and references.
benefits: Information Accuracy: Ensure 100% information accuracy for every transaction
Workflow Clarity: Provide store managers with documentation to confirm payments instantly
Financial Accuracy: Strengthen financial controls and account for all digital transfers
how_to_use: Digital Receipts: Riders can verify payments in the Rider App during transfers
View Proof in BOP: Managers access transaction receipts on the web dashboard
Assessment Logs: Access verifiable digital footprints with timestamps and payment references
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Rider Payments Receipts.eml

whats_new__rider_tags.title: What’s New_ Rider Tags
whats_new__rider_tags.purpose: Upgrade Rider Tags to enable high-speed, effortless organization by moving away from manual management.
whats_new__rider_tags.summary: Rider Tags are digital labels to organize riders based on unique traits, simplifying management and enabling automation.
navigation: Dashboard > Settings > Users & Permissions > Onboarding & KYC
Riders > Rider Name > Rider Overview Modal > Rider name > Edit details
benefits: Better visibility over assets through captured rider characteristics
Standardization and data sanitation with strict formatting rules
Enables reliable reporting and automation across integrated modules
how_to_use: Create tags by assigning a unique name and values in Onboarding & KYC
Assign tags to new riders during creation (Riders > New Rider)
Assign tags to existing riders via Edit details screen
Tags are configured only in Dashboard, not Admin App
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Rider Tags.eml

whats_new__scan___swipe___done_simplifie.title: What’s New_ Scan -_ Swipe -_ Done (Simplified QR FIFO Journey) (1)
whats_new__scan___swipe___done_simplifie.purpose: Simplify rider package pickup process to save time and improve efficiency
whats_new__scan___swipe___done_simplifie.summary: Revamped QR FIFO Flow eliminates redundant steps, provides instant feedback, and streamlines multi-screen workflow for faster package pickups and deliveries
benefits: Higher productivity
Better earnings per hour
Reduced on-ground errors
Frictionless manual entry
Precise error classification
how_to_use: Swipe 'Complete Pickup' to skip intermediate screens
Manually enter order ID if QR code fails to scan
Receive specific error messages for order status
Scan up to 3 orders in batching flow
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Scan -_ Swipe -_ Done (Simplified QR FIFO Journey) (1).eml

whats_new__scan___swipe___done_simplifie.title: What’s New_ Scan -_ Swipe -_ Done (Simplified QR FIFO Journey)
whats_new__scan___swipe___done_simplifie.purpose: Simplify the rider's package pickup process and improve on-ground operations efficiency
whats_new__scan___swipe___done_simplifie.summary: The QR FIFO Flow has been revamped to eliminate redundant steps, reduce time spent on package pickups, and provide a more intuitive and error-free experience for riders.
benefits: Higher productivity
Better earnings per hour
Reduced time spent on pickups
Improved error handling
how_to_use: Swipe 'Complete Pickup' to directly access 'Start for Drop' screen
Manually enter order ID if QR code is damaged or unscannable
Receive specific error messages for pickup status (e.g., Already Picked Up, Order Cancelled)
Scan up to 3 orders in batching flow
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Scan -_ Swipe -_ Done (Simplified QR FIFO Journey).eml

whats_new__search_gets_a_sidekick.title: What’s New_ Search gets a sidekick
whats_new__search_gets_a_sidekick.purpose: Enhance order processing with precision scanning to reduce manual errors and improve efficiency.
whats_new__search_gets_a_sidekick.summary: Introduces a Barcode Scan feature in the 'Update Items' screen to streamline item adjustment by eliminating manual SKU entry.
benefits: Eliminates manual entry mistakes
Reduces item lookup time by up to 70%
Ensures exact SKU adjustment through direct scan-product matching
how_to_use: Look for the new Scanner Icon in your search bar.
Tap the icon, scan the barcode, and the app will instantly pull up the exact product.
Use the scan output to adjust the intended SKU directly.
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Search gets a sidekick.eml

whats_new__smarter_cod_reports_1.title: What’s New_ Smarter COD Reports (1)
whats_new__smarter_cod_reports_1.purpose: Provide deeper visibility into Cash on Delivery (COD) payments collection, transfer, and settlement processes across riders, admins, and bank accounts
whats_new__smarter_cod_reports_1.summary: New COD Orders Collection Report and Online Settlement Report offer enhanced tracking of COD payments, settlement lifecycle, and T+1 remittance flows to improve financial reconciliation and operational efficiency
benefits: Faster and more accurate financial reconciliation
Better visibility into COD and online payment settlements
Easier tracking of rider/admin collected payments
Improved monitoring of T+1 remittance processing
Reduced dependency on manual settlement validation
how_to_use: Access COD Orders Collection Report via COD Management → Get Report
Use Online Settlement Report for accounts with T+1 Remittance enabled
Filter reports by Admin/Rider level for online payment tracking
Monitor transaction/transfer/settlement dates/IDs for reconciliation
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Smarter COD Reports (1).eml

whats_new__smarter_cod_reports.title: What’s New_ Smarter COD Reports
whats_new__smarter_cod_reports.purpose: Provide better visibility into COD collections, online payment settlements, and reconciliation flows
whats_new__smarter_cod_reports.summary: Introducing two new business-critical reports in COD Management to track COD orders, rider/admin collected payments, monitor transfer/settlement statuses, and simplify financial operations for T+1 remittance accounts
navigation: COD Management → Get Report (COD Orders Collection Report)
T+1 Remittance enabled accounts (Online Settlement Report)
benefits: Faster and more accurate financial reconciliation
Better visibility into COD and online payment settlements
Easier tracking of rider/admin collected payments
Improved monitoring of T+1 remittance processing
Reduced dependency on manual settlement validation
how_to_use: Access COD Orders Collection Report via COD Management → Get Report
Use Online Settlement Report for T+1 Remittance enabled accounts

whats_new__smarter_pricing_controls_in_n.title: What’s New_ Smarter Pricing Controls in Network
whats_new__smarter_pricing_controls_in_n.purpose: Enable separate control over what networks charge and pay through External and Internal pricing layers.
whats_new__smarter_pricing_controls_in_n.summary: Introduces dual pricing configuration for networks, allowing independent management of charging and payout rates with a unified pricing engine.
benefits: Independent margin and cost control
Safer and clearer pricing logic
Future-ready financial models
Reduced operational complexity
whats_new__smarter_pricing_controls_in_n.how_to_use: Define External Price (what you charge) and Internal Price (what you pay) in all pricing screens, including Base Pricing, Surge Pricing, and other sections.
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Smarter Pricing Controls in Network.eml

whats_new__systematic_order_network_matc.title: What’s New_ Systematic Order & Network Matching
whats_new__systematic_order_network_matc.purpose: Enhance order and network matching by aligning order properties with network capabilities to prevent incompatible routing.
whats_new__systematic_order_network_matc.summary: This release introduces systematic use of order properties in network matching, ensuring only compatible networks are considered during allocation, reducing manual intervention and operational delays.
benefits: Cleaner and faster network selection
Stronger alignment between order configuration and network capability
Improved operational accuracy at scale
whats_new__systematic_order_network_matc.how_to_use: During order allocation, the system evaluates order properties and filters networks incompatible with Movement and Flow Type requirements, ensuring constraint-driven allocation decisions.
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Systematic Order & Network Matching.eml

whats_new__the_future_of_proactive_fleet.title: What’s New_ The Future of Proactive Fleet Management
whats_new__the_future_of_proactive_fleet.purpose: Integration of AI-powered computer vision and crowdsourced video data for proactive fleet management
whats_new__the_future_of_proactive_fleet.summary: New dashcam integration provides real-time visibility of location and video for fleets, with features like live video streaming and dual-channel front/back visibility to monitor road conditions, driver behavior, and cargo security.
benefits: Live Vehicle Tracking for real-time location and environment visibility
Monitoring Rider Behaviour with geo-tagged video evidence
Last-Mile Clarity for urban delivery environment confirmation
how_to_use: Use Live Video Streaming for high-risk maneuvers and emergency situations
Utilize Dual-Channel Dashcams for simultaneous front/back visibility
Access real-time video feeds through Fleets and Trace platforms
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ The Future of Proactive Fleet Management.eml

whats_new__vas_charge_mis_report.summary: The new VAS Charges Report provides an order-level breakdown of all VAS charges applied during the selected period.

no more guesswork around daily VAS costs
faster reconciliation and billing validation
clear justification of charges to internal teams or end clients
better control over communication-related expenses

whats_new__weekly_digest_1.title: What’s New_ Weekly Digest (1)
whats_new__weekly_digest_1.purpose: Operational resilience and user experience improvements across rider, customer, and order management workflows
whats_new__weekly_digest_1.summary: Updates include rider logout restrictions during active tasks, COD order state protection, AmazonSelfShip ID visibility, Ref ID in SMS, enhanced package code visibility, and blacklisted rider exclusion.
related: Rider Logout Restriction During Active Tasks
COD Order State Protection After Payment Collection
Ref ID Replaces PBID in SMS Communications
Enhanced Package Code Visibility in Rider App
benefits: Prevents accidental rider logout during ongoing deliveries
Maintains task continuity and operational visibility
Reduces disruption to active order fulfillment
Prevents financial reconciliation mismatches
Ensures consistency between payment and delivery status
Eliminates dependency on support teams for ID retrieval
Improves operational efficiency and auditability
Provides consistent visibility of AmazonSelfShip ID
Improves consistency between customer-facing communications and operational workflows
Reduces dependency on multiple order references to verify packages
Ensures orders are only sent to eligible riders
Minimizes manual operational corrections and investigations
how_to_use: System blocks rider logout during active tasks with error popup
COD orders cannot be marked as Failed/Undelivered after payment collection
AmazonSelfShip ID visible in Order Details modal for completed orders
SMS notifications use Ref ID instead of PBID
Package details displayed in structured format with key identifiers
Blacklisted riders excluded from allocation
documents: 💥⚡️ What’s New_ Weekly Digest (1).eml

whats_new__whitelabelled_admin_applicati.purpose: Scale Your Brand with our Infrastructure

benefits: Scale business instantly without massive capital investment
Brand-centric execution
Unified landing page with holistic brand data
Flexible order management
Rider management tools
Cash flow and COD settlement management

Manage orders via flexible order management system

Use cash management tools for COD settlements

cod_limit_for_riders.title: COD Limit for Riders
cod_limit_for_riders.purpose: Give full control over who handles cash, how much they carry, and breach handling with flexible order allocation.
cod_limit_for_riders.summary: Introduces a comprehensive COD management system with controlled allocation, cash limits, enforcement flexibility, and smart alerts for risk control and operational continuity.
benefits: Assign COD orders only to qualified riders
Control cash in the field
Choose strict or partial restriction enforcement
Automate enforcement and reduce manual monitoring
Improve cash reconciliation and reduce leakage risk
how_to_use: Define rider eligibility criteria (lifetime orders, recent performance)
Set cash limits (max outstanding, excess over earnings)
Configure enforcement rules (block all orders or only COD)
Enable smart alerts for threshold breaches
Use automated actions like logging out riders or assigning admin tasks
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ COD Limit for Riders.eml
available_for: M3M Urbana Business Park, Sector 67, Gurugram, Haryana (122101)

dynamic_rider_management_in_networks_1.title: Dynamic Rider Management in Networks (1)
dynamic_rider_management_in_networks_1.purpose: Automate adding/removing riders in networks using Rider Tags to reduce administrative burden.
dynamic_rider_management_in_networks_1.summary: Introduces tag-based network mapping, automatic offboarding, loaned rider management, and flexi shifts to streamline delivery network operations.
benefits: Save hours of admin work
Eliminate human error in rider assignments
Operational flexibility for fleet scenarios
dynamic_rider_management_in_networks_1.how_to_use: 1. Assign tags to riders and link them to networks. 2. Use tags for automatic onboarding/offboarding. 3. Apply loaned rider tags for temporary network access. 4. Manage flexi shifts with tag-based network suspension.
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ Dynamic Rider Management in Networks (1).eml

dynamic_rider_management_in_networks.title: Dynamic Rider Management in Networks
dynamic_rider_management_in_networks.purpose: Automate adding/removing riders in networks using Rider Tags to reduce administrative burden.
dynamic_rider_management_in_networks.summary: Introduces tag-based network mapping, automatic offboarding, loaned rider management, and flexi shifts for streamlined delivery network operations.
benefits: Save Hours of Admin Work
Eliminate Human Error
Operational Flexibility
how_to_use: Link tags to networks; riders are auto-added when tagged
Unassign tags to automatically remove riders from networks
Apply loaned rider tags to temporarily include riders in networks
Flexi Shifts pause tag-bound networks until shifts end
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ Dynamic Rider Management in Networks.eml
dynamic_rider_management_in_networks.available_for: Businesses managing delivery networks via Pidge platform

rider_breaks_1.title: Rider Breaks (1)
rider_breaks_1.purpose: Allow riders to notify businesses when they need breaks, pausing order assignments temporarily to avoid delivery delays and ensure smoother operations.
rider_breaks_1.summary: Rider Breaks enable riders to request short breaks (e.g., meals, rest) during shifts, pausing their order assignments and resuming automatically upon return. This reduces manual intervention, improves rider availability tracking, and enhances operational efficiency.
related: What This Means To YouGemini said
benefits: No more order delays or reassignments due to rider unavailability
Reduced manual operational intervention by admins
Positive impact on rider performance metrics (e.g., acceptance rate)
Accurate rider availability in allocation systems
how_to_use: Set up breaks during roster creation in Riders > Roster & Attendance > Create Roster
Riders request breaks via Executive App: Shift Management > Request Break
Configure peak hours to restrict breaks during high-order-volume periods
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ Rider Breaks (1).eml
available_for: Captive rider fleet (Project Dedicated riders not included in this release)

rider_breaks.title: Rider Breaks
rider_breaks.purpose: Allow riders to notify the business when they need a break, pausing order assignments temporarily and resuming automatically.
rider_breaks.summary: Rider Breaks enable riders to take scheduled breaks without disrupting delivery operations. Breaks pause order assignments and resume automatically, improving efficiency and rider experience.
benefits: No order delays or reassignments due to rider unavailability
Reduced manual admin intervention during rider unavailability
Improved rider performance metrics (e.g., acceptance rate)
Accurate rider availability in allocation systems
how_to_use: Riders request breaks via Executive App: Shift Management > Request Break
Set up breaks during roster creation: Riders > Roster & Attendance > Create Roster
Break duration is set in 5-minute increments based on shift timing
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ Rider Breaks.eml
available_for: Shared riders (Project Dedicated) not supported in this release

rider_flexi_shifts_1.title: Rider Flexi Shifts (1)
rider_flexi_shifts_1.purpose: Introduce a new shift management model for on-demand flexibility
rider_flexi_shifts_1.summary: Flexi Shifts allows admins to broadcast open shift slots, enabling riders to opt-in based on real-time availability, reducing manual effort and improving supply activation during demand spikes.
benefits: Reduces manual effort for fleet admins
Enables dynamic scheduling for riders
Accelerates shift fulfillment with real-time indicators
Improves supply activation during demand spikes
how_to_use: Riders can book open slots up to 4 days in advance via the Rider App
Use Smart Urgency Tags to prioritize shifts
Review order properties (movement type, payment limits, hub distance) before booking
Cancel shifts instantly via the app drawer before logging in
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ Rider Flexi Shifts (1).eml

rider_flexi_shifts_2.title: Rider Flexi Shifts (2)
rider_flexi_shifts_2.purpose: Introduce a brand-new shift management model for on-demand flexibility in workforce scheduling
rider_flexi_shifts_2.summary: Flexi Shifts allows admins to broadcast open shift slots for eligible riders to opt-in based on real-time availability, addressing operational bottlenecks and enhancing agility for both admins and riders.
benefits: Provides flexibility for riders with dynamic working hours
Reduces manual effort for fleet admins
Enables rapid supply activation during demand spikes
how_to_use: Riders can book open slots up to 4 days in advance via the Rider App
View real-time urgency tags like 'Filling Fast' or 'Almost Full'
Access shift details including order types, payment limitations, and hub distances
Cancel shifts instantly via the app before logging in
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ Rider Flexi Shifts (2).eml

rider_flexi_shifts.title: Rider Flexi Shifts
rider_flexi_shifts.purpose: Introduce a new shift management model for on-demand flexibility
rider_flexi_shifts.summary: Flexi Shifts allows admins to broadcast open shift slots, enabling riders to opt-in based on real-time availability, reducing manual effort and improving supply activation during demand spikes.
benefits: Reduces manual effort for fleet admins
Enables quick supply activation during demand spikes
Provides riders with autonomy over earning opportunities
Improves operational scalability for admins
Enhances rider access to open slots without admin intervention
how_to_use: Admins broadcast open shift slots to eligible riders
Riders view and book open slots via the Rider App up to 4 days in advance
Riders receive real-time urgency tags for shift fulfillment
Riders can cancel accepted shifts instantly via the app
Admins scale operations by simultaneously notifying entire rider bases
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ Rider Flexi Shifts.eml

rider_kyc_verification.title: Rider KYC Verification
rider_kyc_verification.purpose: Introducing rider verification directly within the Rider App for streamlined document verification processes
rider_kyc_verification.summary: Rider verification is now integrated into the Rider App, allowing riders to complete Aadhaar, Driving License, and Bank verification with real-time status updates on the dashboard. Admins gain control over verification workflows while reducing manual entry loopholes.
benefits: Reduces operational risk and compliance exposure
Strengthens customer confidence
Enables scalable workforce management
Simplifies admin verification workflows
how_to_use: Riders complete verification via Rider App profile section
Aadhaar verified through DigiLocker
Driving License verified via document upload
Bank verification via Penny drop
Admins approve/reject documents with manual controls
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ Rider KYC Verification.eml

scan___swipe___done_simplified_qr_fifo_j.title: Scan -_ Swipe -_ Done (Simplified QR FIFO Journey)
scan___swipe___done_simplified_qr_fifo_j.purpose: Simplify the rider's package pickup process to save time and improve efficiency
scan___swipe___done_simplified_qr_fifo_j.summary: The QR FIFO Flow has been revamped to eliminate redundant steps, providing a faster and more intuitive experience for riders.
benefits: Higher productivity
Better earnings per hour
Reduced on-ground errors
Faster package pickup

Manually enter order ID if QR code fails
Receive specific error messages for scan issues

smart_routing_and_allocation.title: Smart Routing and Allocation
smart_routing_and_allocation.purpose: Enhance routing efficiency and rider task management through intelligent order merging, dynamic prioritization, and optimized rider allocation.
smart_routing_and_allocation.summary: Introduces forward/reverse order merging, task reprioritization, and Top Rider enhancements to improve operational efficiency and rider flexibility.
benefits: Reduce total distance traveled and operational costs
Enable dynamic task reordering while maintaining SLA compliance
Ensure equitable order distribution through rider-free-time filtering
how_to_use: Combine forward and reverse orders into unified routes
Rearrange live tasks within merged routes
Use 15-minute free-time filter for Top Rider allocation
Prioritize tasks while adhering to guardrails (e.g., no drop before pickup)
documents: 🔔 Coming Soon_ Smart Routing and Allocation.eml
